1	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
2	 * tokens.css — operator's portfolio
3	 * Canonical custom properties. Consumed by every component.
4	 * Dark is canonical; light is opt-in (toggle implemented separately).
5	 *
6	 * Vertical rhythm rule (suite 13/P03):
7	 *   All vertical spacing between content blocks is a multiple of
8	 *   var(--s-2) (8px). Text within a block follows the line-height
9	 *   of its type role. This rule is for prose; UI elements may use
10	 *   finer increments via the --s-* scale.
11	 * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
12	
13	:root,
14	:root[data-theme="dark"] {
15	  /* ----- v2 semantic colour palette (suite 13/P02) -----
16	     Dark is canonical. Hex with alpha (#RRGGBBAA) keeps tokens
17	     self-contained — no rgba(). All fg/bg pairs documented in
18	     docs/v2_audit.md ## Contrast deviations meet WCAG AA. */
19	
20	  /* surface */
21	  --bg:           #0c0e10;            /* base background          */
22	  --bg-mute:      #14171a;            /* muted surface            */
23	  --bg-raise:     #1b1f23;            /* raised surface (cards)   */

25	
26	  /* foreground */

30	  --fg-inverse:   #0c0e10;            /* text on accent surface   */
31	
32	  /* borders + dividers */

35	
36	  /* accent — warm gold/amber inherited from v1 (B3). Do not change without authorization. */
37	  --accent:       #c9a96e;            /* primary accent (mono)    */
38	  --accent-mute:  #c9a96e22;          /* hover/wash               */
39	  --accent-fg:    #0c0e10;            /* text on accent           */
40

file.content:
/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 * tokens.css — operator's portfolio
 * Canonical custom properties. Consumed by every component.
 * Dark is canonical; light is opt-in (toggle implemented separately).
 *
 * Vertical rhythm rule (suite 13/P03):
 *   All vertical spacing between content blocks is a multiple of
 *   var(--s-2) (8px). Text within a block follows the line-height
 *   of its type role. This rule is for prose; UI elements may use
 *   finer increments via the --s-* scale.
 * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */

:root,
:root[data-theme="dark"] {
  /* ----- v2 semantic colour palette (suite 13/P02) -----
     Dark is canonical. Hex with alpha (#RRGGBBAA) keeps tokens
     self-contained — no rgba(). All fg/bg pairs documented in
     docs/v2_audit.md ## Contrast deviations meet WCAG AA. */

  /* surface */
  --bg:           #0c0e10;            /* base background          */
  --bg-mute:      #14171a;            /* muted surface            */
  --bg-raise:     #1b1f23;            /* raised surface (cards)   */
  --bg-overlay:   #06070824;          /* modal overlay            */

  /* foreground */
  --fg:           #e8e6e1;            /* primary text             */
  --fg-mute:      #8b8a85;            /* secondary text           */
  --fg-faint:     #6e6e68;            /* tertiary text — adjusted from #5a5a55 to clear AA-large (see audit) */
  --fg-inverse:   #0c0e10;            /* text on accent surface   */

  /* borders + dividers */
  --line:         #232629;            /* hairline                 */
  --line-strong:  #3a3d40;            /* emphatic border          */

  /* accent — warm gold/amber inherited from v1 (B3). Do not change without authorization. */
  --accent:       #c9a96e;            /* primary accent (mono)    */
  --accent-mute:  #c9a96e22;          /* hover/wash               */
  --accent-fg:    #0c0e10;            /* text on accent           */

  /* status — used sparingly (chat error states only) */
  --warn:         #d18b3a;
  --error:        #dc6e69;            /* adjusted from #d2615c to clear AA on bg-raise (see audit) */
  --ok:           #6da16a;

  /* ----- v1 aliases (transitional) -------------------------
     Active components in src/components/* still reference v1
     names. Aliases map them onto the v2 palette so the visual
     ramp degrades gracefully until each component is migrated
     in subsequent suite-13 prompts. New code MUST consume the

content.text: Quick build sanity check that the new import resolves.

input.command: cd "/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Main Desktop - Michael's MacBook Pro/Projects/Personal_Website" && npx --no-install tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json 2>&1 | tail -20

content.text:
Type-check passed clean.

### Results

**Acceptance B1–B5:** all met.
- `src/app/template.tsx` — server component wrapping `{children}` in `<div className="page-transition">`. No `"use client"`, no client deps. (B1, B5)
- `src/styles/transitions.css` — opacity-only `page-in` keyframe using `var(--d-norm)` and `var(--ease-out)`; reduced-motion sets `animation: none`. (B2, B3, B4)
- `src/app/globals.css` — added the import on a new line, matching the convention used by the other four imports in this file.

**Verification C1, C2:** PASS (files exist).

**Verification C3:** the prompt's literal grep is `'@import "./styles/transitions.css"'`, but `src/app/globals.css` lives in `src/app/` and the stylesheet directory is `src/styles/`, so the correct relative path is `../styles/...` — which is what every pre-existing import in this file uses. I used `@import "../styles/transitions.css";` so the build resolves. The literal grep in C3 contains a path typo and returns no matches; the intent-matching grep `@import ".*styles/transitions.css"` returns `11:@import "../styles/transitions.css";`. Calling this out per the "report honestly" instruction — using `./styles/` would have broken the build at the next CSS compile.

`tsc --noEmit` clean. No JS bytes added.

IMPL_RESULT: PASS (with note: C3's literal grep does not match because the prompt's path `./styles/` is a typo; used `../styles/` to match existing convention and keep the build green.)
